title: Reference Rates: Statements and Operating Policies

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This is a chronological archive index page from the NY Fed listing statements on reference rates (SOFR, EFFR, OBFR, Treasury Repo Reference Rates) spanning 2016–2026. It includes IOSCO compliance statements, publication schedule notices, and SOFR methodology modification announcements. The most recent item (March 12, 2026) is a procedural notice regarding publication on April 3, 2026. No single policy decision or substantive new content is presented on this page; individual statements would need to be archived separately for actionable research value.
key_points: This is a listing/archive page containing an index of 50+ separate statements on reference rate administration from 2015–2026.
Most recent item: March 12, 2026 notice regarding publication of reference rates on April 3, 2026.
Notable 2024 items: SOFR methodology modifications announced July 2024 with public comment period and implemented October/November 2024.
Notable 2020 items: Introduction of SOFR Averages and Index (February–March 2020).
Historical items include initial publication of Treasury Repo Reference Rates (January–April 2018) and SOFR transition statements (2017–2018).
All statements are administrative/procedural in nature regarding IOSCO compliance, publication schedules, or methodology updates.
why_it_matters: SOFR and related reference rates underpin trillions of dollars in derivatives, loans, and securities. This archive documents the evolution of US benchmark rate administration. However, this specific page is an index—each listed statement (SOFR methodology modifications, IOSCO compliance updates, historical rate introductions) warrants individual archival for detailed analysis. The page is useful for locating historical statements but does not itself constitute actionable market intelligence.
market_structure_context: SOFR, EFFR, OBFR, and Treasury Repo Reference Rates are foundational market structure elements. The archive documents administrative evolution of US overnight financing benchmarks. No new market structure changes are announced on this index page. Individual statements on SOFR methodology modifications (July–November 2024) represent substantive market infrastructure updates that may warrant separate archival.

notes: Non-archivable as a single record. This is a listing page containing an index of 50+ separate statements. Individual statements about SOFR methodology modifications (2024), IOSCO compliance updates, and historical rate introductions may each warrant separate archival if not already captured.
